such as himi-slings to prevent shoulder subluxation and 
a hand splint to prevent joint contracture and deformity in the right hand. The services of 
an occupational therapist would be necessary to: 

• 
Assess the patient's needs; 

• 
Develop goals (to be approved by the physician); 

• 
Manufacture or adapt the needed equipment to the patient's use; 

• 
Teach compensatory techniques; 

• 
Strengthen the patient as necessary to permit use of compensatory techniques; and 

• 
Provide
